name: Foaming Facial Cleanser
hero: The reference foaming cleanser for normal-to-oily and combination skin. Ceramides + niacinamide in a pH-balanced base, with the lowest irritation index in the price tier. Boring, replicable, gold-standard.

scoreBreakdown.n: Ceramide complex + MVE delivery — rare in a foaming cleanser.
₹ 950 fair internationally; pricey vs Indian baseline.

ingredients.note: Three-ceramide complex matches stratum corneum ratio.
Modest in a rinse-off product but real.
CeraVe's trademark — extends ceramide deposition slightly.

useCases.b: For normal-to-oily skin. PM after sunscreen and the day's grime.
Non-stripping; pairs cleanly with adapalene or BPO without rebound oil.
Day 4+ after laser or peels — gentle enough, deep enough.

faq.q: Will it dry out my skin?
faq.a: Almost never. The surfactant blend is one of the gentlest at this foaming intensity. If your skin feels tight after, you've over-cleansed (twice in <12 hours, or with hot water).
It's formulation-grade — supports tolerability and trace barrier benefit. For real niacinamide therapy, layer a 5% serum after.

faq.q: Can I use it twice daily?
faq.a: Yes. AM with water, PM with hands — neither requires a Clarisonic-style brush. The brush adds irritation, not benefit.
Hydrating for dry / sensitive / mature; Foaming for normal-to-oily / acne-prone. Same family, picked by skin oil baseline, not season.

sources.n: Draelos ZD et al. Ceramide-based cleansers in barrier repair. JDD 2018.
Mukhopadhyay P. pH and the acid mantle. Indian J Dermatol 2011.
Ananthapadmanabhan KP et al. Surfactant-induced skin damage. Int J Cosm Sci 2004.
